title: Reverse Mountain Goes Dark
description: On pages 1–3, reverse mountain goes dark as the crew reaches Reverse Mountain and is dragged into the impossible currents that lead to the Grand Line.
scene: Reverse Mountain Goes Dark. The crew reaches Reverse Mountain and is dragged into the impossible currents that lead to the Grand Line. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Nami, this is bad!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: The light just went out!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: And that was our guiding light too! What do we do now?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Well it is only a lighthouse. It can go out from time to time.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Besides, it's for times like these you have a Navigator, you know?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: So don't worry. I know the basic direction at least.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Wow, you're amazing.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: More importantly, can you please get off from there!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: No, this is my spot! You can't have it!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: When did I say I wanted it!?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: But this is rather troubling...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: At this rate, it'll be exactly as the rumours say...!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: The entrance to Grand Line,

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: is a mountain.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: A mountain!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Exactly. I had a hard time believing it myself when I looked at the map,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But look!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: The light of guidance was directly pointing at reverse mountain located right here on Red Line.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Does that mean we have to crash through the mountain?

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Present location

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Waterway

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Reverse Mountain

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: No, there's a Waterway right here.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: A Waterway? That's crazy!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: REVERSE MOUNTAIN

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Even if that Waterway really is there, there's no way a ship could climb up a mountain!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But that's what the map says.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That's right. As if what Nami says could ever be wrong!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: But isn't that the map you stole from buggy? How dependable could it be?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Are we really gonna climb up a mountain on a ship!? That sounds soooo cool!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: So it's a magic mountain, huh?

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on reverse mountain goes dark. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
